skill.name: daily-news-report
skill.description: Generates daily Markdown news reports by scraping configured technology, AI, productivity, and essay sources.
skill.summary: Collects configured web sources and turns selected items into a daily Markdown news report.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static high-severity weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by ordinary words and field names, not cryptographic code. Markdown code fences were also misclassified as Ruby backtick execution. The confirmed risk is medium because the skill intentionally fetches external web pages, uses browser tooling, writes reports and cache files, and processes untrusted web content.

medium_findings.title: External Web Fetching and Browser Automation
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to fetch public websites with WebFetch and browser tooling. This is expected for the feature, but it exposes the agent to untrusted web content and external site behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools and source configuration clearly show intentional network and browser access. The URLs are public news sources, so this is a legitimate feature with elevated exposure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted Page Content Enters Agent Prompts
medium_findings.description: Subagents are instructed to scrape web pages and return structured summaries. The instructions do not include explicit safeguards to ignore prompt-like instructions embedded in fetched pages.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The task prompts process external page content directly. No evidence of malicious intent was found, but prompt-injection resistance is not documented in these instructions.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem Writes for Reports and Cache Updates
medium_findings.description: The skill writes generated Markdown reports and updates cache data. This is necessary for the workflow, but users should run it where writing NewsReport output and cache updates is acceptable.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file output and cache update behavior is explicitly described. The target files are local report and cache paths, so the risk is bounded but real.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings appear to match ordinary text such as description fields and sorting language. No cryptographic API, hash algorithm use, or credential handling was found at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows metadata text, prose, and configuration labels. There is no executable cryptographic code at those locations.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Ruby Backtick Execution Alerts
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick execution findings are Markdown code fences, inline code, and examples. They are documentation blocks, not executable Ruby or shell backtick syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are inside Markdown fences or prose examples. The skill does not contain Ruby source code that would execute these backticks.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Public Source URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are public source lists used for news collection. This is expected behavior, but source reputation and availability should be reviewed periodically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each cited URL is part of the documented source configuration or examples. The destinations are not secret endpoints or obvious exfiltration targets.

low_findings.title: Limited Shell Command Allowlist
low_findings.description: The skill permits Bash only for mkdir, date, and ls patterns. These commands support directory creation, dating, and listing, but command access should remain constrained.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools line explicitly limits Bash to narrow command patterns. This is not evidence of command injection, but it remains a permission to monitor.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Alerts
low_findings.description: The cited lines describe checking cache.json and reusing cached content to avoid duplicate scraping. No host enumeration, user discovery, or system inventory behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows cache-related workflow text only. The lines do not instruct the agent to inspect operating system details or enumerate local resources.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Access to Configured Websites
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses WebFetch and browser automation against configured external websites. Treat fetched page content as untrusted input.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Network access is explicit in the tool permissions and source list. The behavior is legitimate for a news report skill but should be disclosed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Browser Rendering of External Pages
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill can open JavaScript-rendered pages through Chrome DevTools MCP. This increases exposure to dynamic third-party pages.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The browser phase and browser source tier are documented directly. No malicious browser action is shown, but dynamic rendering has a larger attack surface.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Report and Cache Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow writes Markdown reports and cache state. Users should understand which local files are created or updated.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The report path and cache structure are explicitly documented. The locations do not show arbitrary file targeting.

content.value_statement: Technical teams need a fast way to review useful AI, engineering, product, and productivity updates. This skill collects configured sources, filters low-value items, and writes a daily Markdown report.
actual_capabilities: Reads source configuration from sources.json and prioritizes tiered sources.
Fetches configured web pages with WebFetch or browser rendering when needed.
Filters collected items by quality, topic relevance, and duplicate checks.
Generates a dated Markdown report under a NewsReport output directory.
Updates cache metadata for processed URLs, content fingerprints, and run history.
limitations: It depends on external websites that may change layout, block access, or require login.
It does not verify every claim in fetched articles independently.
Browser rendering requires the Chrome DevTools MCP tools to be available.
Report quality depends on the configured source list and model judgment.

use_cases.title: Engineering Team Briefing
use_cases.description: Create a daily technical digest that highlights relevant AI, developer, and product updates for team review.

use_cases.title: Research Inbox Reduction
use_cases.description: Summarize high-signal sources into one Markdown report instead of checking many websites manually.

use_cases.title: Founder Market Scanning
use_cases.description: Track product launches, essays, and productivity ideas that may affect product strategy or positioning.

prompt_templates.title: Run Today Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate today's daily news report using the configured sources.

prompt_templates.title: Run Specific Date
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the daily news report for 2026-06-30 and save it with the standard Markdown format.

prompt_templates.title: Focus on AI Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a daily report and prioritize AI research, developer tools, and practical engineering updates.

prompt_templates.title: Review Source Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the report, then summarize which configured sources failed, produced weak items, or should be reviewed.

output_examples.input: Generate today's report from the configured sources.
output_examples.output: A dated Markdown report with a summary section, curated news items, source links, keywords, scores, and generation metadata.

output_examples.input: Generate a report focused on developer tools and AI papers.
output_examples.output: A focused digest that ranks relevant developer tools, AI papers, and practical engineering posts above general productivity items.

output_examples.input: Run the report and note source failures.
output_examples.output: A completed report plus a short status note listing failed sources, skipped sources, and any degraded execution mode.

best_practices: Review sources.json before first use and remove sources that are not relevant to your workflow.
Run the skill in a workspace where generated reports and cache updates are expected.
Check source failures periodically because website layouts and access rules change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated summaries as verified facts without checking original links.
Do not run browser scraping against websites where you lack permission or violate terms.
Do not add sensitive internal URLs to sources.json unless the workspace is approved for that access.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a dated Markdown news report from configured public web sources.

faq.question: Can I change the sources?
faq.answer: Yes. Edit sources.json to add, disable, or reprioritize sources before running the skill.

faq.question: Does it need browser tools?
faq.answer: Only some sources need browser rendering. The skill can use simpler WebFetch paths when pages allow it.

faq.question: Does it write files?
faq.answer: Yes. It writes reports to NewsReport and updates cache data used for deduplication and history.

faq.question: Is the content verified?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes fetched sources, so important claims should be checked against the original pages.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill fetches external web pages, can render dynamic pages, and writes local report and cache files.
